metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1087
metadata.comment: Rationale: Quantitative proteomic comparisons indicates an enrichment of MAL2 in synaptic vesicles that harbour vGlut-1 and would store glutamate. An antibody that shows specificity for MAL-2 based on peptide antigen competition is used. Western blotting of synaptic subcellular fractions, immunofluoresence co-localization with synaptic vesicle markers, immunogold electron labelling of purified synaptic vesicles and within intact tissue all evidence MAL-2 as a bona-fide synaptic vesicle membrane protein.. Experimental description: Rat tissue is largely used as explicitly stated in Methods: Male Wistar rats (300-350g) but figures mention mouse hippocampal and cerebellar sections (e.g. figure 8). Citation to previous papers in the immunoisolation section of materials and methods would suggest that immuno-isolation of synaptic vesicles used rat as starting material.

checks.label: OWL Consistency
